content:
# Mark Chapter 2 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 32r–32v (PDF pages 63–64) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_002_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_002_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ When he returned to Capernaum some days later, word spread that he was at home.

² So many gathered that there was no room left, not even at the door, and he was speaking the word to them.

³ Then some men came, bringing to him a paralytic carried by four of them.

⁴ Since they could not bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, they removed the roof above where he was, and having dug through it, they lowered the mat on which the paralytic lay.

⁵ When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, "Child, your sins are forgiven."

⁶ Now some of the scribes were sitting there, reasoning in their hearts,

⁷ "Why does this man speak this way? He blasphemes! Who can forgive sins but God alone?"

⁸ At once Jesus, perceiving in his spirit what they were thinking, said to them, "Why do you reason these things in your hearts?

⁹ Which is easier—to say to the paralytic, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Rise, take up your mat, and walk'?

¹⁰ But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins"—he said to the paralytic—

¹¹ "I say to you, rise, take up your mat, and go home."

¹² And he rose, immediately took up the mat, and went out before them all. They were all astonished and glorified God, saying, "We have never seen anything like this!"

¹³ He went out again beside the sea, and the whole crowd came to him, and he taught them.

¹⁴ As he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth, and said to him, "Follow me." And he rose and followed him.

¹⁵ While Jesus was reclining at table in Levi's house, many tax collectors and sinners were dining with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many who followed him.

¹⁶ When the scribes of the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they said to his disciples, "Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?"

¹⁷ When Jesus heard this, he said to them, "Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners."

¹⁸ Now the disciples of John and the Pharisees were fasting. And people came and asked him, "Why do the disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not?"

¹⁹ Jesus said to them, "Can the wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.

²⁰ But the days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast on that day.

²¹ No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. Otherwise, the new patch pulls away from the old, and the tear becomes worse.

²² And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. Otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the skins are ruined. New wine is for new wineskins."

²³ One Sabbath he was passing through the grain fields, and his disciples began to pluck heads of grain as they walked.

²⁴ The Pharisees said to him, "Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?"

²⁵ He said to them, "Have you never read what David did when he was in need and hungry, he and those with him—

²⁶ how he entered the house of God, in the time of Abiathar the high priest, and ate the bread of the Presence, which only the priests were permitted to eat, and shared it with his companions?"

²⁷ Then he said to them, "The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.

²⁸ Therefore the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath."

---
## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**Strong agreement across all three translators.** Mark 2 presents no major textual variants between Sinaiticus and Vaticanus. The translators diverge only in style.

1. **κράβαττος (vv. 4, 9, 11, 12):** EMET: "pallet." LOGOS: "mat." PNEUMA: "stretcher." I adopted LOGOS's "mat" as the best balance—accurate to the colloquial Greek term and clear in English.

2. **"Son of Man" (vv. 10, 28):** All three translators agree on this rendering. This is the first appearance in Mark of Jesus's signature self-designation.

3. **υἱοὶ τοῦ νυμφῶνος (v. 19):** EMET: "sons of the bridal chamber." LOGOS: "wedding guests." PNEUMA: "wedding guests." I followed the majority with "wedding guests"—the idiom needs translation, not transliteration.

4. **"Scribes of the Pharisees" (v. 16):** All three correctly read Sinaiticus's specific phrase ΟΙ ΓΡΑΜΜΑΤΕΙΣ ΤΩΝ ΦΑΡΙΣΑΙΩΝ rather than the broader "scribes and Pharisees." This is an important distinction—these are scribes who belonged to the Pharisaic movement.

5. **"Abiathar the high priest" (v. 26):** All three translators preserve the reading without emendation. EMET and LOGOS note the historical difficulty with 1 Samuel 21. This is the correct approach—translate what the manuscript says and note the issue.

6. **v. 27 — "The Sabbath was made for man":** EMET: "came-into-being for the sake of man." LOGOS: "was made for man." PNEUMA: "was made to serve people." I followed LOGOS—it is both faithful and memorable. PNEUMA's "serve" is interpretive but captures the force well.

No ⚠️ disagreements for this chapter.

— SOPHIA, AI Translator, in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA
